CVE-2024-27980.2c21d71ae746e16d465a7171a7595b9eec88132c:
debug-adapter: pass shell:true when resolved path is a batch file

The previous commit resolved bare command names via PATH/PATHEXT but
still spawned the resolved path directly. That fixes the bare-name
lookup but not the CVE-2024-27980 hardening: Node's ProcessWrap::Spawn
rejects any options.file ending in .bat/.cmd with EINVAL regardless of
whether the path is absolute. The check is suffix-based — see
node:src/util-inl.h IsWindowsBatchFile.

For the reported npm-wrapper scenario — where bun.cmd is the only
discoverable entry on PATH — the resolved path is C:\...\bun.cmd and
spawn still fails.

Change resolveCommand to return { command, useShell } and have the
adapter pass shell: useShell to spawn. With shell:true Node rewrites
the file to cmd.exe and wraps the invocation as /d /s /c "...", so
the batch-file check in the C++ layer sees cmd.exe and succeeds.

Expanded the regression test to assert useShell is set correctly for
bare names, absolute paths, and reversed PATHEXT orderings.
CVE-2024-27980.dafbb6b7f0003f76f568cac1fc0319766bf8bac6:
debug-adapter: resolve command via PATH/PATHEXT on Windows

child_process.spawn on Windows does not apply PATHEXT resolution, and
post-CVE-2024-27980 it refuses to launch .cmd/.bat files unless shell:
true is set. Bun installed via the npm wrapper on Windows lands on PATH
as bun.cmd, so the debug adapter's default spawn("bun", ...) call fails
with EINVAL.

Resolve bare command names to an absolute .exe/.cmd path before
spawning. Avoids the shell: true escaping surface and keeps ENOENT
error messages intact when the command is genuinely missing.

Fixes #29636

CVE-2023-4863.ed75c88a5ca1b54345083721a0dd185ed61dedc5:
Bun.Image — Sharp-shaped image pipeline (jpeg/png/webp, resize, rotate, modulate) (#30032)

## What

`Bun.Image`: decode → transform → encode for JPEG/PNG/WebP/GIF/BMP (+
HEIC/AVIF/TIFF on macOS/Windows) with no native module install.
Statically links the reference codecs and a highway-SIMD resize kernel;
system backends (ImageIO+vImage, WIC) for the OS-native formats.

```ts
await Bun.file("photo.jpg")
  .image()
  .resize(1024, 1024, { fit: "inside" })
  .rotate(90)
  .webp({ quality: 85 })
  .write("thumb.webp");

return new Response(new Bun.Image(upload).resize(200).jpeg());
```

## Benchmarks vs sharp 0.34.5

CI release build (`bunx bun-pr 30032`), 50 iters, linux/x64,
`sharp.concurrency(1)`:

| op | Bun.Image | sharp | speedup | ΔRSS bun | ΔRSS sharp |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| `metadata()` | **0.004 ms** | 0.28 ms | **70×** | 0 MB | 2.5 MB |
| 1080p PNG → resize 400×400 inside → jpeg q80 | **28.6 ms** | 39.5 ms |
**1.38×** | 0 MB | 8.9 MB |
| 1080p PNG → resize 800×600 → webp q80 | **82.7 ms** | 110.1 ms |
**1.33×** | 0 MB | 2.5 MB |
| 1080p PNG → jpeg q80 (no resize) | 34.3 ms | 33.8 ms | 0.98× | 8.4 MB
| 12 MB |
| 1080p JPEG → resize 400×400 inside → jpeg | **10.2 ms** | 12.5 ms |
**1.22×** | 0 MB | 0 MB |
| 1080p JPEG → resize 200×200 inside → webp | **10.6 ms** | 11.8 ms |
1.12× | 0 MB | 0 MB |
| 1080p JPEG → webp q80 | **175 ms** | 208 ms | **1.19×** | 1.4 MB | 7
MB |
| **4K** JPEG → 800×450 → jpeg | **35.8 ms** | 45.5 ms | **1.27×** | 0.5
MB | 0 MB |
| **4K** JPEG → 1920×1080 → jpeg | **57.2 ms** | 69.9 ms | **1.22×** |
12 MB | 21 MB |
| **12MP** JPEG → 400×300 → jpeg | **34.3 ms** | 39.4 ms | **1.15×** |
0.5 MB | 0 MB |
| **12MP** JPEG → 1024×768 → webp | **138 ms** | 165 ms | **1.20×** |
3.1 MB | 8.4 MB |

(x64 with libjpeg-turbo C-only — no NASM yet; arm64 has NEON wired so
JPEG is better still. Reproduce: `cd bench/image && bun install && bunx
bun-pr 30032 && bun-30032 bench-resize.mjs --sharp`.)

Why it's faster: i16 fixed-point resize (`ReorderWidenMulAccumulate` →
PMADDWD/SMLAL, 2× lanes vs f32); JPEG decodes at the smallest M/8 IDCT
scale ≥ target; `metadata()` is a sync header read; constructor borrows
the ArrayBuffer (`pin()` per-task) instead of memcpy; output bytes go
straight to JS via the codec's own free, no dupe; resize scratch is one
caller-allocated arena, zero `malloc` in the kernel.

[Visual side-by-side
gist](https://gist.github.com/Jarred-Sumner/8d91b281650b9d75028afb3799c1c3f0)
— 12 real photos × 4 resize configs × 3 formats, sharp left / Bun.Image
right.

## Surface

- **Input**: path string · ArrayBuffer/TypedArray (borrowed zero-copy,
pinned per-task; resizable rejected) · Blob/BunFile/S3File · `data:` URL
· `Bun.file(…).image()` / `Blob#image()`
- **Chainable** (fixed-slot `Pipeline`; setters overwrite, not stack):
`.resize(w, h?, {filter, fit, withoutEnlargement})` ·
`.rotate(90|180|270)` · `.flip()` · `.flop()` · `.modulate({brightness,
saturation})` · `.jpeg({quality, progressive})` ·
`.png({compressionLevel, palette, colors, dither})` · `.webp({quality,
lossless})` · `.heic({quality})` · `.avif({quality})`
- **Filters** (full Sharp set + MKS): `nearest` `box` `bilinear` `cubic`
`mitchell` `lanczos2` `lanczos3` `mks2013` `mks2021`
- **Terminals** (off-thread except metadata): `.bytes()` · `.buffer()` ·
`.blob()` · `.toBase64()` · `.dataurl()` · `.placeholder()` (thumbhash)
· `.metadata()` · `.write(dest)`
- **Body init**: `new Response(image)` / `new Request({body: image})`
encode and set `Content-Type`
- **Options**: `{maxPixels}` decompression-bomb guard (header-checked
pre-alloc, all codecs + system backends + resize output), `{autoOrient}`
EXIF Orientation, `{backend: "auto"|"system"|"bun"}`

## Dependencies

| lib | version | license | build | CVE status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| libjpeg-turbo | 3.1.4 | BSD-3/IJG/zlib | DirectBuild, 8-bit; arm64
NEON intrinsics on, x64 C-only | clean (rdbmp.c stubbed) |
| libspng | 0.7.4 | BSD-2 | DirectBuild, single TU, vendored zlib | zero
ever assigned (OSS-Fuzz since inception) |
| libwebp | 1.6.0 | BSD-3 | DirectBuild, full enc/dec/dsp/sharpyuv,
per-file -mavx2/-msse4.1 | clean (CVE-2023-4863 fixed in 1.3.2) |

`LICENSE.md` updated. `src/image/README.md` documents layout +
how-to-extend.

## System backends

| | macOS (ImageIO + vImage) | Windows (WIC) | Linux |
|---|---|---|---|
| JPEG/PNG/WebP | static codecs (output identical across platforms) |
static codecs | static |
| GIF/BMP | static `codec_gif.zig`/`codec_bmp.zig` | static | static |
| **TIFF** | ✅ decode | ✅ decode | `UnsupportedOnPlatform` |
| **HEIC** | ✅ dec+enc 10.13+ | ✅ dec+enc (HEIF Image Extensions;
quality wired via IPropertyBag2) | `UnsupportedOnPlatform` |
| **AVIF** | ✅ dec; enc on Apple Silicon only (Intel has no AV1 encoder)
| ✅ dec+enc (AV1 Video Extension) | `UnsupportedOnPlatform` |

CoreGraphics dispatch lives entirely in C++
(`image_coregraphics_shim.cpp`); decode goes through
`vImageBuffer_InitWithCGImage` for straight-alpha (no premul roundtrip),
wrapped in an explicit autoreleasepool per WorkPool call. WIC encode
threads `ImageQuality`/`HeifCompressionMethod` through a C++ shim so the
SDK's own VARIANT layout is authoritative. Symbol resolution stays lazy
(dlsym/CoCreateInstance), zero startup cost.

## Tests — 204 across four suites

`image.test.ts` (~80) — happy path: per-format round-trip, all 9
filters, rotate/flip, fit:inside/withoutEnlargement, EXIF auto-orient,
modulate, palette+dither PNG, compressionLevel,
`.buffer()/.blob()/.toBase64()/.write()`, `new Response(image)`,
`Bun.file().image()`, GIF/BMP/TIFF, HEIC/AVIF sniff + per-platform
encode.

`image-adversarial.test.ts` (~58) — format confusion (lying magic bytes,
polyglots), truncation sweep at every byte offset, single-byte XOR fuzz
at every offset, hostile header dimensions (0×0, 2³¹-1, 2³²-1), zlib
bombs in IDAT, hostile EXIF IFD0, hostile BMP BITFIELDS / biSize wrap,
**GIFLIB-CVE-2016-3177-style LZW** (code > avail), 255-byte GIF
sub-block, lossless roundtrip on LCG noise, rotate⁴=id, leak plateau
under repetition, throwing Proxy on every option object, re-entrant
getters, detached/resizable ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, `data:` URL,
100 concurrent terminals.

`image-kernels.test.ts` (~38) — algorithm pins: flat-field DC gain (all
9 filters), 1×1 upscale, same-size identity, mitchell monotone over a
step, **nearest downscale stays discrete**, impulse spread ordering,
anisotropy, FS dithered ramp tracks source mean, FS determinism, 1×N/N×1
dither edge cases.

`image-vs-sharp.test.ts` (29) — pixel-level cross-reference against
pre-baked Sharp/libvips output. mitchell/bilinear/cubic/lanczos2:
MAE<0.5 max≤5 (centre/support math correct). lanczos3/mks*: MAE<1.0
max≤16 (libvips quantises offsets to 64 bins; we compute exact per-pixel
weights — both correct). Hard-edge sources omitted by design (those
measure phase/pre-shrink, not kernel).

## Known follow-ups

- x64 libjpeg-turbo NASM SIMD — `nasm` not in build images; arm64 NEON
is on.
- `new Response(image)` encodes on the calling thread; a `.Locked` body
would stream from the WorkPool.
- Integer pre-shrink for non-JPEG would close the remaining gap on huge
PNG/WebP downscales.

---------

Co-authored-by: autofix-ci[bot] <114827586+autofix-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

GHSA-JFHR-4V9P-9RM4.ede635b8a921e95f0d847641a48e4d448baf9a7f:
fix(install): store tarball integrity hash in lockfile for HTTPS dependencies (#27018)

## Summary
- HTTPS/URL tarball dependencies were not having their integrity hash
stored in the lockfile, allowing a malicious server to change the
tarball contents without detection on subsequent installs
- Now computes a sha512 hash from the downloaded tarball bytes during
extraction and stores it in both the binary lockfile and text bun.lock
- The hash is verified on re-download, matching the behavior of npm
registry packages
- Old lockfiles without integrity hashes continue to work (backward
compatible)

## Changes
- `src/install/integrity.zig`: Added `Integrity.forBytes()` to compute
sha512 from raw bytes
- `src/install/install.zig`: Added `integrity` field to `ExtractData`
struct
- `src/install/PackageManagerTask.zig`: Compute hash from tarball bytes
for both remote and local tarball tasks
- `src/install/PackageManager/processDependencyList.zig`: Set
`package.meta.integrity` from computed hash
- `src/install/lockfile/bun.lock.zig`: Serialize/deserialize integrity
for `remote_tarball` and `local_tarball` types

## Test plan
- [x] Integrity hash is stored in text lockfile for tarball URL deps
- [x] Integrity hash is consistent/deterministic across reinstalls
- [x] Integrity mismatch (changed tarball content) causes install
failure
- [x] Old lockfiles without integrity still install successfully
(backward compat)
- [x] Fresh installs produce integrity hashes
- [x] All 12 existing tarball tests pass (no regressions)
- [x] Tests fail with `USE_SYSTEM_BUN=1` (confirms fix is effective)

Fixes GHSA-jfhr-4v9p-9rm4

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

GHSA-PFWX-36V6-832X.f5d98191b7c0a605a4ee79c5e37a2933046728be:
fix(install): store and verify SHA-512 integrity hash for GitHub tarball dependencies (#27019)

## Summary

- Compute SHA-512 hash of GitHub tarball bytes during extraction and
store in `bun.lock`
- Verify the hash on subsequent installs when re-downloading, rejecting
tampered tarballs
- Automatically upgrade old lockfiles without integrity by computing and
persisting the hash
- Maintain backward compatibility with old lockfile format (no integrity
field)

Fixes GHSA-pfwx-36v6-832x

## Lockfile format change

```
Before: ["pkg@github:user/repo#ref", {}, "resolved-commit"]
After:  ["pkg@github:user/repo#ref", {}, "resolved-commit", "sha512-..."]
```

The integrity field is optional for backward compatibility. Old
lockfiles are automatically upgraded when the tarball is re-downloaded.

## Test plan

- [x] Fresh install stores SHA-512 integrity hash in lockfile
- [x] Re-install with matching hash succeeds
- [x] Re-install with mismatched hash rejects the tarball
- [x] Old lockfile without integrity is auto-upgraded with hash on
re-download
- [x] Cache hits still work without re-downloading
- [x] Existing GitHub dependency tests pass (10/10)
- [x] Existing git resolution snapshot test passes
- [x] Yarn migration snapshot tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

CVE-2018-7160.7f57c9c74733f39da5557c6bf73edee4634e87d7:
fix(debugger): use crypto.randomUUID() for WebSocket auth token

Replace Math.random().toString(36) with crypto.randomUUID() for generating
the debugger WebSocket path token. Math.random() is not cryptographically
secure and produces predictable output, making it possible for local
attackers to guess the token and connect to the debugger via Chrome DevTools
Protocol. This is the same class of vulnerability as Node.js CVE-2018-7160.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
